name: setup-dev-env
description: 開発環境セットアップスキル（依存関係インストール、DB初期化、環境変数設定）
capabilities: Checks Java, Node.js, pnpm, Docker, and Docker Compose versions
Installs backend dependencies using Gradle wrapper
Starts PostgreSQL database with Docker Compose
author: Crearize
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown documentation for development environment setup procedures. No executable code, scripts, or network operations exist. The bash commands shown are illustrative examples within code blocks, not runtime code. All static findings are false positives: hidden file references are read-only ls commands for verification, cryptographic algorithm alerts are scanner artifacts from Japanese text, and external command alerts are documentation code blocks that cannot be executed.
valueStatement: Manual development environment setup is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates dependency installation, database initialization, and environment configuration for consistent, reproducible development environments.
limitations: Does not install runtime dependencies (Java, Node.js, Docker must be pre-installed)
Does not modify system settings or global configurations
Does not handle custom database schemas beyond Flyway migrations
Assumes standard project structure with backend and frontend directories

useCases.title: Onboard quickly
useCases.description: Get new developers productive on day one with automated, consistent environment setup

useCases.title: Standardize environments
useCases.description: Ensure all team members have identical development environments across machines

useCases.title: Reduce setup time
useCases.description: Eliminate manual environment configuration overhead for new project starts

promptTemplates.title: Full setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up the complete development environment for this project using the setup-dev-env skill. Include backend dependencies, frontend dependencies, and database setup.

promptTemplates.title: Backend only
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up only the backend development environment. Run Gradle build and verify database connectivity.

promptTemplates.title: Database reset
promptTemplates.prompt: Reset the database environment using Docker Compose and run all Flyway migrations from scratch.

promptTemplates.title: Quick verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify the current development environment status. Check all tool versions and confirm dependencies are properly installed.

bestPractices: Run the skill after cloning a fresh repository to ensure all dependencies are installed
Verify environment variables match between backend/.env and frontend/.env.local
Use the full setup workflow when onboarding new team members
antiPatterns: Running setup without checking pre-installed runtime versions first
Skipping database setup and expecting the application to work
Modifying environment files while the database container is running

faq.question: What tools must be installed before running this skill?
faq.answer: Java 21+, Node.js 20+, pnpm, Docker, and Docker Compose must be pre-installed on your system.

faq.question: Can I run only specific phases of the setup?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify target parameters for backend-only, frontend-only, database-only, or both configurations.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my global environment settings?
faq.answer: No, this skill only operates within the project directory and does not modify system-wide configurations.

faq.question: Is my data safe when running database migrations?
faq.answer: Flyway migrations run on the Docker PostgreSQL container. Your existing local databases are not affected.

faq.question: What if the build fails during setup?
faq.answer: The skill reports failures at each phase. Common fixes include verifying Java/Node versions and checking Docker is running.

faq.question: How is this different from Docker Compose alone?
faq.answer: This skill orchestrates the entire workflow including dependency installation, builds, migrations, and verification.
